zh.Bourbon, Allier, Auvergne, France: 法國奧維阿利也布爾波-利阿哈波奧爾特
zh.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon於1130年出生在法國奧維阿利也布爾波-利阿哈波奧爾特。她和Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier至少有1個女兒。她去世於1156年在她的家鄉享年26歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bourbon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon was born in 1130, in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, France. She had at least 1 daughter with Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. She died in 1156, in her hometown, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon est née en 1130, à Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, France. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. Elle est décédée en 1156, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 26 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon wurde 1130 in Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich geboren. Sie hatte mit Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1156 im Alter von 26 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon è nata il nel 1130 nel Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, Francia. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. Morì nel 1156, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 26 anni.

ja.Bourbon, Allier, Auvergne, France: フランスアウバーグンアリールボウルボン-ル'アルチャンバウルト
ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbonは1130年にフランスアウバーグンアリールボウルボン-ル'アルチャンバウルトで生まれた。彼女はGuillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquierとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1156年に故郷で26歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon(은)는 1130년 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 부르보-'아르챔바트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1156년 그녀의 고향에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon nasceu em 1130, em Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, França. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. Ela faleceu em 1156, em sua cidade natal, com 26 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon родилась в 1130 г. в местности бурбон-л'арчамбаулт, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. Она умерла в 1156 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 26 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Marguerite de Bourbon nació en 1130, en Bourbon-l'Archambault, Allier, Auvergne, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Guillaume IV DE FORCALQUIER Comte de Forcalquier. Murió en 1156, en su pueblo, a la edad de 26 años.
